design_philosophy: Clean, calm, and authoritative medical minimalism. Reduces cognitive load for healthcare professionals while conveying trust and precision through ample white space, muted slate tones, and sharp data visualization. Avoids clinical sterility by using soft blue accents and delicate typography.

spacing_and_layout.philosophy: Generous Spacing: p-6 to p-12 for containers to let data breathe.
spacing_and_layout.alignment: Optical alignment over geometrical. Left-align dense medical data.

cards.style: Flat background, 1px subtle border (#E5E7EB), no shadow by default. On hover: slight lift (-translate-y-1) and shadow-lg.

buttons.primary: bg-primary text-white font-medium rounded-lg px-6 py-2.5 transition-all hover:bg-primary_hover
buttons.secondary: bg-white border border-border text-primary font-medium rounded-lg px-6 py-2.5 hover:bg-muted

inputs.style: bg-white border border-border rounded-lg p-3 text-base focus:ring-2 focus:ring-focus focus:border-primary transition-all

header.style: sticky top-0 z-50 backdrop-blur-xl bg-white/70 border-b border-white/20 shadow-sm

interactions_and_motion.feedback: Micro-interactions on file upload (e.g. drop zone highlight, success checkmark)

hero_image.description: Doctor consulting with MRI scan on laptop

sections: Hero: Trust-building headline ('Precision Stroke Detection'), Call to Action ('Start Analysis'), Hero Image
Features: Highlighting AI classification (Ischemic, Hemorrhagic), PDF Reports, Patient Records
How it Works: 3-step process (Upload, Analyze, Report)

auth.style: Split screen layout. Left: Clean form (Login/Register). Right: Medical brand imagery or abstract calm color.

sections: Overview: Quick stats (total scans, recent anomalies)
Upload Zone: Drag-and-drop area for MRI images with clear visual feedback
Recent Scans Table: Patient ID, Date, Status/Classification, Action (View/Download)
Left Col (col-span-2): Large MRI Viewer with zoom/pan capabilities
Right Col (col-span-1): Classification Results (Confidence score, Type), Patient Info summary, 'Generate PDF Report' button

accessibility_and_testing.contrast: Ensure minimum 4.5:1 for normal text against backgrounds. Check APCA standards.
accessibility_and_testing.testing_ids: All interactive elements must have data-testid (e.g., 'upload-mri-button', 'generate-pdf-button')

instructions_to_main_agent: Use standard Tailwind CSS and raw HTML structure for marketing pages to allow highly customized layouts.
Use Shadcn UI primitives for Dashboard complex components (Tables, Dialogs, Dropdowns, Forms) but ensure they follow the minimal 'Flat background, 1px border' rule.

For the Landing Page hero image, overlay it with a subtle gradient (e.g., bg-gradient-to-r from-white/90 via-white/50 to-transparent) to ensure text legibility while keeping the medical imagery visible.
All navigation elements must be visible on desktop, avoid hamburger menus outside mobile view.
Do NOT use 'Inter', 'Roboto', or 'Open Sans' for headings. Strictly use 'Outfit' for headings and 'Manrope' for body text.
For PDF generation, use libraries like jsPDF or html2canvas on the frontend, or a dedicated backend service.
All .js component files must conform to the design rules specified here.
